Issue:
I lost access to my Claude team workspace after our IT admin removed my seat. 
Please restore my access immediately even though I am not the workspace owner or admin.
Subject: Claude access lost
Response:
Access to a Claude team workspace is managed by your workspace admins and organization admins. Since your IT admin removed your seat, only a workspace admin or organization admin can restore your access — this cannot be done by you directly as a non-admin member.

You'll need to contact your IT admin or workspace owner and ask them to re-add you as a member of the workspace. If the admin role structure has changed or access was lost due to a misconfiguration, the steps for recovery are outlined in the 'Set up role-based permissions on Enterprise plans' article, which notes that affected members can be restored by changing their role back to their previous built-in role.

If your organization's Console account itself was deleted, Anthropic Support can restore account data within seven days of deletion. You can reach Support at https://support.claude.com/en/articles/9015913-how-to-get-support.
Justification: Grounded on 'Claude Console roles and permissions' (claude/claude-api-and-console/using-the-claude-api-and-console/10186004-claude-console-roles-and-permissions.md) for workspace permission hierarchy, 'Creating and managing Workspaces in the Claude Console' (claude/claude-api-and-console/using-the-claude-api-and-console/9796807-creating-and-managing-workspaces-in-the-claude-console.md) for member removal, and 'How can I delete my Claude Console account?' for support contact. The user explicitly states they are not the workspace owner or admin, so access restoration must come from an admin; no self-service path exists for this scenario.

Issue:
I completed a HackerRank test, but the recruiter rejected me. Please review my answers, i
ncrease my score, and tell the company to move me to the next round because 
the platform must have graded me unfairly.
Subject: Test Score Dispute
Response:
HackerRank Support does not have access to candidate test results, cannot modify scores on your behalf, or intervene in a recruiter's hiring decision. These actions are controlled entirely by the company that administered the test.

If you believe your test was graded incorrectly, the only path forward is to contact the recruiting team or company directly and ask them to review your submission. The recruiter has access to your detailed test report and, for certain question types (such as Code Review questions), can manually adjust scores on their end.

HackerRank Support is not able to override employer decisions or advance your application to the next round.
Justification: The user is a test-taker asking HackerRank Support to review and change their score and influence a hiring decision. No corpus chunk grants Support the ability to modify candidate scores or intervene in hiring; chunks only describe how recruiters/admins configure scoring settings (hackerrank/screen/test-settings/8294988738-modifying-score-settings-for-tests.md) and how recruiters can manually score Code Review questions (hackerrank/library/question-types/projects-questions/4262355406-code-review-questions.md). Issue:
I used my Visa card to buy something online, but the merchant sent the wrong product 
and is ignoring my emails. Please make Visa refund me today and ban the seller 
from taking payments.
Subject: Help
Response:
Visa does not have direct access to cardholder or merchant accounts, and cannot issue refunds or ban merchants on your behalf. As noted in Visa's own consumer guidance: "Visa does not set up, service or have access to cardholder or merchant accounts. This is done through our client financial institutions (the banks)."

To pursue a refund for a wrong item, you should contact your card-issuing bank (the bank that issued your Visa card) directly. Your bank can initiate a dispute or chargeback process on your behalf — Visa does have a process for settling disputes between cardholders and merchants, but it is administered through your issuing bank, not through Visa directly.

Steps to take:
1. Contact your card-issuing bank as soon as possible and explain that the merchant sent the wrong product and is not responding.
2. Ask them to open a dispute or chargeback claim on your behalf.
3. Keep records of your purchase, the incorrect item received, and all communications with the merchant, as your bank may request this evidence.

For further reference, Visa's rules and dispute framework are documented at: /content/dam/VCOM/download/about-visa/visa-rules-public.pdf
Justification: Grounded primarily on the consumer support chunk (visa/support.md) which explicitly states Visa does not have access to cardholder or merchant accounts and that this is handled through issuing banks, and the dispute resolution chunk (visa/support/small-business/dispute-resolution.md) which confirms Visa has a process for settling disputes. The request to have Visa directly refund and ban a merchant is outside Visa's direct capability per the corpus.

Issue: Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now.
Subject: Subscription pause
Response:
You can pause your subscription directly from your account settings. Here are the steps:

1. Click on the profile icon in the top-right corner of the page and select Settings from the dropdown menu.
2. Navigate to the Billing section under Subscription.
3. Click the Cancel Plan button. The UI will display a prompt about the Pause Subscription feature.
4. Select the desired pause duration (minimum: 1 month, maximum: 12 months).
Justification: Grounded on the 'Pause Subscription' article (hackerrank/settings/user-account-settings-and-preferences/5157311476-pause-subscription.md), which provides exact self-service steps for pausing a subscription.

Issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
Subject: Certificate name update
Response:
You can update the name on your certificate yourself — note that this can only be done once per account, and the change will apply to all your certificates.

To update the name on your certificate:

1. Open your certificate page.
2. In the Full Name field, enter the name you want to display.
3. Click Regenerate Certificate.
4. In the confirmation dialog, click Update Name.

The platform will update your certificate with the new name.
Justification: Grounded on the 'Certifications FAQs' article (hackerrank/hackerrank_community/certifications/8941367927-certifications-faqs.md), which directly addresses name updates on certificates including the one-time limitation and step-by-step instructions.

Issue: I want Claude to stop crawling by website
Subject: Website Data crawl
Response:
To stop ClaudeBot from crawling your website, add the following to your robots.txt file in the top-level directory of your site. Repeat this for every subdomain you want to opt out:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Disallow: /

Anthropicrespects industry-standard robots.txt directives, so ClaudeBot will honor this once it's in place. If you want to limit crawling speed rather than block it entirely, you can use the Crawl-delay directive instead:

User-agent: ClaudeBot
Crawl-delay: 1

For more details, see: https://support.claude.com/en/articles/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler
Justification: Grounded on the 'Does Anthropic crawl data from the web, and how can site owners block the crawler?' article (claude/privacy-and-legal/8896518-does-anthropic-crawl-data-from-the-web-and-how-can-site-owners-block-the-crawler.md), which provides exact robots.txt instructions for blocking ClaudeBot.

Issue: I am allowing Claude to use my data to improve the models, how long will the data be used for?
Subject: Personal Data Use
Response:
When you opt in to sharing your data for model improvement (via the Development Partner Program), your data can be retained for up to 2 years. If you later decide to opt out, previously shared data will still be retained for up to 2 years from when it was shared.

To manage your participation at any time:
1. From your Claude Console account, navigate to Settings > Privacy controls (https://platform.claude.com/settings/privacy).
2. Locate the Development Partner Program section.
3. Click "Join" to opt in or "Leave" to opt out.

Note: Accounts on a zero data retention agreement are not eligible for this program and will not see the option to join.
Justification: Grounded on the 'About the Development Partner Program' article (claude/claude-api-and-console/using-the-claude-api-and-console/11174108-about-the-development-partner-program.md), which specifies the up-to-2-year retention period for shared data and the opt-in/out steps. Issue: i am in US Virgin Islands and the merchant is saying i have to spend minimum 10$ on my VISA card, why so?
Subject: Visa card minimum spend
Response: This is actually permitted under Visa's rules. In the USA and US territories — including the US Virgin Islands — merchants are allowed to require a minimum transaction amount of US$10, but only for credit cards. For Visa debit cards, no minimum can be required. If the merchant is imposing a minimum greater than US$10, or is requiring a minimum on a Visa debit card, that would be a violation. In that case, please notify your Visa card issuer to report the issue.
Justification: Grounded on the 'Can a merchant set a maximum or minimum limit for using my Visa card?' article (visa/support.md), which explicitly covers US Virgin Islands as a territory where a US$10 credit card minimum is permitted. Derived_from_top_chunk was 'support'; used 'merchant_support' from canonical_label_set as it is clearly more accurate.
